questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.A lipped cylindrical glass container for laboratory use.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Beaker”.Beaker: a lipped cylindrical glass container for laboratory use. Vase: a container that is used for holding cut flowers. Aquarium: a tank or pool or bowl filled with water for keeping live fish. Atrium: a large open central area in a public or commercial building.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that gives the most appropriate meaning of the BOLD idiom.It is good to bear in mind all the important factors before making a decision.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Ponder”.Bear in mind: be mindful of or consider something. Ponder: think about (something) carefully, especially before making a decision or reaching a conclusion. Abandon: cease to support or look after (someone); desert.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the BOLD segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.“An interview after the campaign ends is the normal for every candidate and every campaigner in every election,” he said.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “in the norm”.“is continuality” does not fit in the context of the statement and “is the normally” is a grammatically incorrect phrase. The + noun (norm).

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Comradeship
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Lonesomeness”.Comradeship: the company and friendship of others with common aims. Lonesomeness: causing a feeling of loneliness.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Given below are four jumbled sentences. Select the option that gives their correct order.A. First impressions count, and the first impression that a potential employer will have of you is going to depend on how you present your resume. B. The statistics are that the average employer only spends a few seconds looking at it, so it is imperative that it makes an impression. C. Nobody can ever underestimate how important it is to have a good resume. D. This is going to be your one and only chance to capture a potential employer’s attention, or for resume to be tossed into the file of those they don’t want to pursue.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘CABD’.The right sequence is: C. Nobody can ever underestimate how important it is to have a good resume. A. First impressions count, and the first impression that a potential employer will have of you is going to depend on how you present your resume. B. The statistics are that the average employer only spends a few seconds looking at it, so it is imperative that it makes an impression. D. This is going to be your one and only chance to capture a potential employer’s attention, or for resume to be tossed into the file of those they don’t want to pursue.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.No sooner had / I started the movie / then the power / cut off.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “Then the power”.No sooner always follow by than. Correct sentence: no sooner had I started the movie than the power cut off.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice.Trumpets are being blown by the opposition to announce the victory.
options: The opposition blow the trumpets to announce the victory.
The opposition are blowing the trumpets to announce the victory.
The opposition blew the trumpets to announce the victory.
The opposition were blowing the trumpets to announce the victory.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The opposition are blowing the trumpets to announce the victory”.Present continuous tense: Active voice passive voice Subject + is/ am/ are + V (Ing) + object object + being + is /am /are + V3 + by + object The opposition are blowing the trumpets to announce the victory Trumpets are being blown by the opposition to announce the victory.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank.A metrical thinker should always be moving in a direction which is beneficial and have always been right at every __________.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “stride”.Stride: step The most appropriate answer is “stride”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in active voice.How many aspirants had been interviewed by him before lunch?
options: How many aspirants had he interviewed before lunch?
How many aspirants has he interviewed before lunch?
How many aspirants did he interview before lunch?
How many aspirants was he interviewing before lunch?
questions.explanation: The correct answer is A.The given sentence is in passive voice of past perfect tense. Active voice passive voice Subject + had + V3 + object object + had + been + V3 + by + subject. How many aspirants had he interviewed before lunch? How many aspirants had been interviewed by him before lunch?

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the Incorrectly spelt word.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B.Atheist: a person who disbelieves in the existence of God. Amateur: a person who takes part in an activity for pleasure, not as a job. Allegiance: loyalty or commitment to a superior or to a group or cause. Annually: once a year

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. If there is no need to substitute it, select ‘No substitution required’.Cyber crime have been in the news from the nineteen eighties.
options: has been on the news since
has been in the news from
has been in the news since
questions.explanation: The correct answer is D.Given sentence is in Present perfect continuous tense. The subject is singular therefore, singular verb will come here “has been”. So, the correct answer is “has been in the news since”.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that can be used as a one-word substitute for the given group of words.A host who introduces performers
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C.Compere: A person who introduces the performers or contestants in a variety show. Presenter: Someone who introduces or leads programs, particularly on television or at events. Introducer: someone or something that introduces. Host: a person who receives or entertains other people as guests.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that expresses the given sentence in indirect speech.The old woman said to me, “Life has taught me some hard lessons.”
options: The old woman tells me that life had taught her some hard lessons.
The old woman told me this that life has had taught her some hard lessons.
The old woman tells me that life is teaching her some hard lessons.
The old woman told me that life had taught her some hard lessons.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option D.Said to changed into told. Has changed into had. So, the correct answer is “The old woman told me that life had taught her some hard lessons.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.Condemn
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option C.Condemn: express complete disapproval of (censure, criticize). Forgive: stop feeling angry or resentful towards (someone) for an offence, flaw. Or mistake. Disappoint: fail to fulfil the hopes or expectations of. Praise: express warm approval or admiration of. Prevent: keep (something) from happening.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.impending
questions.explanation: The correct answer is Option A.Impending: of an event regarded as threating or significant) about to happen; forthcoming. Imminent: about to happen. Distant: far away in space or time. Ignorant: lacking knowledge or awareness in general; unsophisticated. Unyielding: (a person or their behavior) unlikely to be swayed; resolute.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the option that gives the most appropriate meaning of the underlined idiom.I am working on too many projects. I think I have ____________________.
options: bitten off more than I can chew
questions.explanation: The correct answer is C.Bite off more than one can chew: take on a commitment one cannot fulfil. Nail in the coffin: event or action seriously harming something. Make a mint: make a very large amount of money. Make light of: treat as unimportant.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.He explained that the pillars of tribalism / that humans rely on for security would always / counter any significant / culture or social change.
options: He explained that the pillars of tribalism
that humans rely on for security would always
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B.There is a error in “culture or social change”. Here, ‘cultural’ will come here instead of ‘culture’.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} Select the most appropriate synonym of the given word.Ruck
questions.explanation: The correct answer is B.Ruck: it is a situation where a group of people are fighting or struggling. Straighten: make or become straight. Mass: involving a large number of people or things. Unbend: make or become straight from a bent or twisted form of position.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.The recently hike / in the price of petroleum products / will hit / household budgets severely.
options: in the price of petroleum products
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “The recently hike”.Here, ‘The recent hike’ should be used instead of ‘The recently hike’. Adjective ‘recent’ should be used instead because it modifies the noun ‘the price’. The correct sentence is ‘The recent hike in the price of petroleum products will hit household budgets severely.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error.your cousin’s name / precedes before mine / in the / admission list.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is “precedes before mine”.Preposition ‘before’ superfluous or not required here. The verb ‘precedes’ means ‘to be or go before something or someone, as in place, order, importance, rank, or time. Correct sentence: your cousin’s name precedes mine in the admission list.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘catches’.The most appropriate word is ‘catches’. The given passage is in the present tense. Thus, the correct answer is catches.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘So’.However: used to introduce a statement that contrasts with or seems to contradict something that has been said previously. Also: in addition; too. Either: used before the first of two (or occasionally more) given alternatives (the other being introduced by or).

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘for’.The most appropriate word for the given blank is ‘for’. The correct answer is for.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘parts’.The most appropriate word is ‘parts’.

questions.question: img, svg {vertical-align: middle; max-width:100%; } *{overflow-wrap: break-word;} In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank.How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: The correct answer is ‘Acting’.The most appropriate word is ‘Acting’. The given context means ‘to perform in a play or film’.
